CVE-2019-20637.title: An issue was discovered in Varnish Cache before 6.0.5 LTS, 6.1.x  ...
CVE-2019-20637.description: An issue was discovered in Varnish Cache before 6.0.5 LTS, 6.1.x and 6.2.x before 6.2.2, and 6.3.x before 6.3.1. It does not clear a pointer between the handling of one client request and the next request within the same connection. This sometimes causes information to be disclosed from the connection workspace, such as data structures associated with previous requests within this connection or VCL-related temporary headers.

CVE-2020-11653.title: An issue was discovered in Varnish Cache before 6.0.6 LTS, 6.1.x  ...
CVE-2020-11653.description: An issue was discovered in Varnish Cache before 6.0.6 LTS, 6.1.x and 6.2.x before 6.2.3, and 6.3.x before 6.3.2. It occurs when communication with a TLS termination proxy uses PROXY version 2. There can be an assertion failure and daemon restart, which causes a performance loss.

CVE-2021-36740.title: Varnish Cache, with HTTP/2 enabled, allows request smuggling and  ...
CVE-2021-36740.description: Varnish Cache, with HTTP/2 enabled, allows request smuggling and VCL authorization bypass via a large Content-Length header for a POST request. This affects Varnish Enterprise 6.0.x before 6.0.8r3, and Varnish Cache 5.x and 6.x before 6.5.2, 6.6.x before 6.6.1, and 6.0 LTS before 6.0.8.

CVE-2022-23959.title: In Varnish Cache before 6.6.2 and 7.x before 7.0.2, Varnish Cache ...
CVE-2022-23959.description: In Varnish Cache before 6.6.2 and 7.x before 7.0.2, Varnish Cache 6.0 LTS before 6.0.10, and and Varnish Enterprise (Cache Plus) 4.1.x before 4.1.11r6 and 6.0.x before 6.0.9r4, request smuggling can occur for HTTP/1 connections.

CVE-2022-45060.title: An HTTP Request Forgery issue was discovered in Varnish Cache 5.x ...
CVE-2022-45060.description: An HTTP Request Forgery issue was discovered in Varnish Cache 5.x and 6.x before 6.0.11, 7.x before 7.1.2, and 7.2.x before 7.2.1. An attacker may introduce characters through HTTP/2 pseudo-headers that are invalid in the context of an HTTP/1 request line, causing the Varnish server to produce invalid HTTP/1 requests to the backend. This could, in turn, be used to exploit vulnerabilities in a server behind the Varnish server. Note: the 6.0.x LTS series (before 6.0.11) is affected.

CVE-2024-30156.title: Varnish Cache before 7.3.2 and 7.4.x before 7.4.3 (and before 6.0 ...
CVE-2024-30156.description: Varnish Cache before 7.3.2 and 7.4.x before 7.4.3 (and before 6.0.13 LTS), and Varnish Enterprise 6 before 6.0.12r6, allows credits exhaustion for an HTTP/2 connection control flow window, aka a Broke Window Attack.

CVE-2025-30346.title: Varnish Cache before 7.6.2 and Varnish Enterprise before 6.0.13r1 ...
CVE-2025-30346.description: Varnish Cache before 7.6.2 and Varnish Enterprise before 6.0.13r10 allow client-side desync via HTTP/1 requests.

CVE-2025-47905.title: Varnish Cache before 7.6.3 and 7.7 before 7.7.1, and Varnish Ente ...
CVE-2025-47905.description: Varnish Cache before 7.6.3 and 7.7 before 7.7.1, and Varnish Enterprise before 6.0.13r14, allow client-side desync via HTTP/1 requests, because the product incorrectly permits CRLF to be skipped to delimit chunk boundaries.
